description: Task-type labels describing what the student is required to do in a data structures or algorithms exam question.

label_policy.primary_definition: The main intellectual output expected from the student.
label_policy.secondary_definition: Supporting reasoning required to justify or analyze the primary output.

labels.name: Design Data Structure
labels.description: Construct or propose a data structure supporting specified operations and constraints.

labels.name: Design Algorithm
labels.description: Design an algorithm to solve a computational problem.

labels.name: Prove Correctness
labels.description: Prove or rigorously justify that an algorithm or data structure is correct.

labels.name: Analyze Complexity
labels.description: Analyze time and/or space complexity (worst-case, average, expected, or amortized).

labels.name: Amortized Analysis
labels.description: Prove amortized bounds using aggregate, accounting, or potential methods.

labels.name: Solve Recurrence
labels.description: Solve or analyze a recurrence relation.

labels.name: Asymptotic Reasoning
labels.description: Reason about asymptotic relationships, growth rates, or Big-O/Theta laws.

labels.name: Prove Lower Bound
labels.description: Prove a lower bound or impossibility result under a specified model.

labels.name: Construct Counterexample
labels.description: Disprove a claim by constructing a concrete counterexample.

labels.name: Trace or Simulate Execution
labels.description: Simulate or trace the execution of an algorithm or data structure on a given input.

labels.name: Augment Existing Data Structure
labels.description: Modify or extend an existing data structure while preserving performance guarantees.

labels.name: Select Answer
labels.description: Choose correct option(s) in multiple-choice or true/false format.
